subject: Sociology
title: Antiracism as Practice
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Antiracism as Practice.

hook.text: Not being racist isn't enough, Ibram X. Kendi argues. If you're not actively fighting racism, you're allowing it.

buildup.text: Antiracism is the active practice of opposing racial inequity through changed policies, not just changed hearts.

discovery.text: Kendi's framework: any policy producing racial inequity is racist. Any policy reducing it is antiracist.

twist.text: The binary is uncomfortable. It rejects the safe middle ground of 'not racist' that most people claim.

climax.text: Antiracism shifts the focus from individual attitudes to measurable outcomes. It asks: what do the numbers show?

punchline.text: The opposite of racism isn't neutrality. It's antiracism.

quiz.question: What does Kendi's antiracism framework focus on?
options: Policies that produce or reduce racial inequity
